hypothesis: Incorporating an active learning loop into the tensor completion framework—where the surrogate model iteratively selects the next most informative lattice structure to experimentally validate based on uncertainty estimates derived from the tensor factor matrices—will reduce the number of required experiments to achieve a target R² threshold by at least 30% compared to passive biased sampling, because the low-rank structure of the tensor explicitly encodes inter-mode correlations that can identify maximally informative unobserved entries across the design space.
